questions.text: Which of the following is a Lewis base?
questions.explanation: Ammonia has a lone pair of electrons available to donate, fitting the Lewis definition of a base.

questions.text: What is the pH of a 0.01 M NaOH solution?
Which of the following is the conjugate base of H2PO4-?
questions.explanation: A conjugate base is formed when the acid (H2PO4-) loses one proton (H+).

questions.text: A buffer solution is prepared by mixing:
questions.explanation: A buffer requires a weak acid/base and its corresponding salt (conjugate pair).

questions.text: The pH at the equivalence point of a titration between a strong acid and a weak base is:
questions.explanation: The salt produced undergoes hydrolysis to form an acidic solution.

questions.text: In a Galvanic cell, the electrode where oxidation occurs is the:
questions.explanation: Oxidation always occurs at the anode electrode.

questions.text: During the electrolysis of molten NaCl, which product is formed at the cathode?
questions.explanation: Sodium ions (Na+) are reduced to sodium metal (Na) at the cathode.

questions.text: Which of the following is used as an electrolyte in a dry cell (Leclanché cell)?
questions.explanation: Ammonium chloride serves as the electrolyte in traditional dry cells.

questions.text: The main raw material for the manufacture of sulfuric acid by the Contact Process is:
questions.explanation: Sulfur or iron pyrites are burned to produce the starting SO2 gas.

questions.text: Which catalyst is used in the Haber Process for the synthesis of ammonia?
questions.explanation: Finely divided iron with molybdenum as a promoter is used in ammonia synthesis.

questions.text: Which of the following is a nitrogenous fertilizer?
questions.explanation: Ammonium sulfate contains nitrogen, an essential plant nutrient.

questions.text: Which of the following is a thermoplastic polymer?
questions.explanation: Thermoplastics like polyethylene can be melted and reshaped repeatedly.

questions.text: The monomer of natural rubber is:
questions.explanation: Natural rubber is a polymer made from isoprene units (2-methyl-1,3-butadiene).

questions.text: Which polymer is formed by condensation polymerization?
questions.explanation: Nylon-6,6 is formed by the condensation of adipic acid and hexamethylenediamine, releasing water.

questions.text: The functional group of an ester is:
questions.explanation: The ester group consists of a carbonyl group adjacent to an ether linkage.

questions.text: The reaction between an alcohol and a carboxylic acid to form an ester is called:
questions.explanation: This specific condensation reaction is known as Fischer esterification.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct name for CH3CH2OCH2CH3?
questions.explanation: Diethyl ether is the common name, and ethoxyethane is the IUPAC name.
